meta.goal: Build github.com/Autocss-com/autocss = the refined, completed version of `starter`. Measuring stick: the finished app must look, behave, and work like `dhcp` (the working prototype), changing nothing except to improve to the D7460N Architecture. Separate repo so the two originals are never touched.

repos.autocss: this repo - best-of-both: starter's architecture, completed using dhcp as the target, every feature rewritten to D7460N.
repos.starter: the D7460N-correct rewrite of dhcp; CSS-driven visibility done, but incomplete. Architecture reference.
repos.dhcp: messy-but-complete working prototype; the FEATURE + LOOK reference (an MVP, a floor not a ceiling).

rules: CLAUDE.md in this repo is canonical and non-negotiable.
HTML+CSS do everything; JS is CRUD-only (API transport).

JS's single UI bridge is `oninput` on state-machine <input>s - fired by BOTH user action AND programmatically (initial load/restore = select the nav radio: set .checked=true + input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})) so the input's OWN oninput fires). NEVER click/onclick/change/addEventListener/dynamic import(). dispatchEvent is the SINGLE SANCTIONED exception: used ONLY to make a programmatically-selected nav radio fire its own oninput on load/restore (no real user event exists there); all real user interactions fire oninput natively - no dispatch.
No data-*/class/id; drive dirty/valid/enabled via CSS :has(:valid)/:has(:invalid)/:checked/:empty/:not(:empty)/aria-disabled.
JSON -> elements via toTagName() is allowed; no innerHTML string-building for presentation.
No utils/helpers/utility file names - name each module for what it DOES.
Semantic clarity beats cleverness; modern ES6+ baseline; stateless/idempotent; one API base.
Size: smaller is a guideline not a rule; D7460N compliance may need more code - if autocss ends up larger than dhcp, explain why.
meta.heuristic: Per feature (HTML + companion CSS + companion JS together, companion tracking mandatory): (1) what does it do; (2) is there a MORE MODERN native HTML/CSS way - if yes, STOP and ASK the user before changing markup/CSS, every time (identifying != applying); if no, keep unchanged; (3) will the universal 'one UI to rule them all' app ever need it - YES is the overwhelming default = KEEP & MODERNIZE. Dropping is a rare exception: only if truly redundant, and ALWAYS confirm with user first. Partial/half-built features: inspect, default to COMPLETE not drop.
meta.verify: Enabled env: network=Full (data hosts + Playwright CDN), browser=Playwright chromium via setup script. Tests = JS-logic node unit tests + static/compliance checks + Playwright DOM/behavioral/regression. Pixel-perfect bleeding-edge CSS fidelity needs a HUMAN eye (user does this manually).
meta.cadence: File-by-file, feature-by-feature. Stop and ask on ANY doubt. Check in before FIRST push and after each numbered build step. Test each feature + run regression as we go. Keep companions in lockstep.
meta.principle: USER PRIORITY (restated + elevated 2026-06-15): do the complex things everyone else over-engineers, but in SIMPLE, direct, declarative-first, browser-first, non-complex ways => easy, fast, small, cheap, interoperable. LESS complexity, NEVER more. Every change must REDUCE complexity, not add it; when in doubt the simpler/more-declarative path wins.
future_goals: POST-MVP interoperability demo: build reference instances embedding this ONE UI into each major CMS + framework (WordPress, Drupal, React, Vue, Angular, etc.) to prove it interoperates with the over-engineered/complex systems. ALL instances serve ONE shared instance of this UI from a CDN. The point: declarative-first/browser-first interoperates with everything AND is simpler/faster/smaller/cheaper.
Color-theme selection (next session): persist + restore exactly like nav and color-scheme (state-machine selection -> browser storage).

REBUILD transitions.css as ONE self-contained, drop-in-anywhere transition system (like color-scheme.css). Current one fades elements in at different times + some more than once (observed 2026-06-16); it is not a single independent system. Want: declarative, browser-first, single consistent fade-in on init + on tab/content change, fired once. Currently commented out in index.html (temporary test state).

COLOR-SYSTEM MIGRATION (Phase 1->2): migrate the app's color CONSUMERS (layout.css/a11y.css/typography.css — --bg-active/--txt-*/--bg-panel/--button-bg/row 5% currentcolor) to the Phase-1 CONTRACT names (--fg/--bg/--bg-selected/--bg-hover/--fg-accent/--outline), and convert themes.css into color-theme-<bluehex>.css (OKLCH overrides of those SAME contract names, linked AFTER color-scheme.css). Then the natural CSS cascade does everything: theme present -> themed; theme absent -> Phase-1 system-color fallback. This is the CASCADE model, NOT an @layer/alias 'bridge' (my earlier mischaracterization, corrected 2026-06-16). See meta.color_system.

MODERN-WEB-GUIDANCE PERIODIC REVIEW (advisory): periodically reconcile the GoogleChrome/modern-web-guidance resource (Claude Code plugin) against autocss's current features/capabilities and proactively SUGGEST improvements it surfaces. Advisory only — subordinate to CLAUDE.md + SESSION-HANDOFF; on conflict repo docs win; identify != apply (confirm with user before any change).

radii_.css (WIP, parked 2026-06-21 per user): the border-radius scale is incomplete. Renamed radii.css -> radii_.css (trailing underscore = inactive per CLAUDE.md; it was never linked in index.html anyway). Revisit: finish it as a drop-in radius system and re-activate (drop the underscore + link it after the other layers) when ready.

DEMO ECOSYSTEM (real, diverse products — NOT a 'gallery'; user-reframed 2026-06-25): one repo PER demo in the autocss-com org, each a REAL website/web-app with its OWN dataset, layout, workflow, and user-experience; collectively they prove AutoCSS adapts to ANY of them. NO umbrella repo (the diversity IS the point). NAMING: `autocss-<backend>` (user-chosen). DELIVERY: light-DOM web component (CONFIRMED 2026-06-25; NOT iframe — rejected for a11y + security). DECIDED demos: (1) `autocss-vanilla` = the CURRENT architecture AS-IS = international.dance (wife's ballet studio; international); (2) Pokemon web-app (for son); (3) crime-statistics, location-based reporting + alerting (Fairfax County + Virginia State Police); (4) Bible-study web-app; (5) psychiatrist website. Pokemon + crime-stats + Bible-study REUSE the SAME Holy Grail triage layout + workflow as the current app. HOSTING MODEL (answered 2026-06-25): each repo SELF-HOSTS its static front-end on GitHub Pages (custom domains, e.g. international.dance) — the D7460N SPA is 100% static so Pages is a perfect fit for the UI. BUT GitHub Pages is STATIC-ONLY (no server, no DB, no secrets): the dynamic layers — user accounts/login + secure area, saved favorites + notes (Bible/Pokemon/crime-stats), and payments + retail store (international.dance) — require an EXTERNAL hosted backend (auth + DB + payment processor). The static SPA reaches it via the EXISTING CRUD JS transport (oninput -> api.js), exactly as D7460N already mandates (JS = transport to external APIs; data never lives in the repo / on Pages). The 'backend' is never the repo and never Pages. 3rd-party DATA APIs (a Bible API, PokeAPI, a crime-stats API) are read CLIENT-SIDE; whether each can be called DIRECTLY vs needs a THIN PROXY depends on its CORS + auth/key requirements — BACKEND-PER-DEMO IS RESEARCH-PENDING (user has some Bible API URLs to share; Claude to research each source's requirements, then map demo -> data source -> direct-call-or-proxy). SHARED GENERIC FEATURE MODULES (the reason for the naming discipline): build each cross-cutting capability ONCE as a drop-in, single-concern, intuitively-named module reusable across demos — authentication/login, account/secure-area, favorites, notes, cart, checkout/payments, store/catalog — so a feature built for one demo drops into another with no rework. Tracked as issues (label `demo`); issues #33-#39 are the framework-named placeholders to be reconciled with the decided products once backend-per-demo is researched. USER creates + maintains org repos (Claude is repo-scoped to autocss-com/autocss; cannot create org repos). DATA SOURCES per demo (user-confirmed 2026-06-25): see meta.demo_data_sources — Pokemon=PokeAPI (+sprite pictures), Bible=AO Lab bible.helloao.org, crime=Fairfax FCPD ArcGIS (+FBI CDE +data.virginia.gov); all DIRECT browser-callable (no proxy) EXCEPT copyrighted Bible translations (ESV/API.Bible) + VSP's no-API portal. BACKEND MAPPING (confirmed 2026-06-25): psychiatrist -> autocss-wordpress; Pokemon -> autocss-vue; crime-stats -> autocss-angular (swapped from Drupal per user — Angular suits the location data-dashboard + alerting app, and user has never used Drupal); Bible-study -> autocss-react. RESERVED (unassigned, for future products incl. the user's not-yet-named 'and more'): autocss-drupal, autocss-joomla, autocss-sharepoint. Each backend consumed HEADLESS — read data stays on the external APIs (meta.demo_data_sources); the backend supplies only the integration narrative + auth/accounts + saved favorites/notes (+ commerce where relevant). Issues #33-#39 reframed to these real products / reserved 2026-06-25.

AGENTS.md ADOPTION (cross-assistant canonical doc; user-directed 2026-06-25): mirror github.com/D7460N/starter — make AGENTS.md the SINGLE canonical, AI-AGNOSTIC source of truth that ALL assistant files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, .agents/, Copilot/Cursor/etc.) POINT to, instead of CLAUDE.md being canonical. Live-checked starter 2026-06-25: it HAS a canonical AGENTS.md ('All AI tool files ... are pointers to this file', ~15 sections, AI-agnostic) BUT its CLAUDE.md is STILL the full ~4200-word standalone rules doc that never became a pointer -> the port was STARTED but NEVER FINISHED (user suspected exactly this). DO IT COMPLETELY here: (1) author AGENTS.md = a complete AI-agnostic port of CLAUDE.md's canonical rules; (2) reduce CLAUDE.md to a thin pointer to AGENTS.md; (3) add .cursorrules / .agents pointers as in starter. Tracked as a GitHub backlog issue. WHY: one canonical doc every AI assistant reads -> no drift across per-tool files. Own session.

color_system.what: An INDEPENDENT, drop-in-anywhere MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM built in phases. Plain CSS cascade; no JS for color values; no special bridge mechanism.
phase_1_color-scheme.css: Standalone file (assets/css/color-scheme.css) that works BY ITSELF with no other CSS/JS.

JOB 1 = rename user-agent/system defaults into intuitive --fg (foreground)/--bg (background) variables (+ link/accent/selected/hover/disabled/muted/outline) that devs can remember. It sets NO literal color VALUES — every token defaults to a CSS SYSTEM COLOR (Canvas/CanvasText/AccentColor/...), so it adapts to OS light/dark + forced-colors for free.
JOB 2 = it is the FALLBACK when no color-theme file is present; the page still looks right on system colors alone.
JOB 3 = it DEFINES the variable NAMES a color-theme file later OVERRIDES via the natural CSS cascade.
Also owns the light/dark MECHANISM: :root{color-scheme: light dark} (Rule 19, system-follow) + the 3-state Light/Dark/System radio override via pure :has().
CONSUMERS must reference these contract names — that is how a theme (or the system-color fallback) reaches the whole app.
phase_2_color-theme-??????.css: Filename color-theme-??????.css where ?????? = the 6-digit HEX of the theme's base color (hex used in the FILENAME for brevity only).
Contents = ONLY CSS variable OVERRIDES of the Phase-1 contract names. Colors written in OKLCH (not hex) so the ENTIRE palette derives from the ONE base color via hue/lightness/alpha.

Override mechanism = NATURAL CSS CASCADE: link the color-theme file AFTER color-scheme.css in <head>; being later it wins. No @layer needed, no cross-file reference — a later file overrides an earlier one just by load order (a CSS superpower).
FIRST theme = the dhcp BLUE. Today's themes.css (the OKLCH palette) is what becomes color-theme-<bluehex>.css.
Swap themes by swapping which color-theme-*.css is linked; remove all theme files -> Phase-1 system-color fallback.
color_system.phase_3_controls: HTML controls / 4x4 hue picker that reads hues from linked color-theme-*.css filenames ([href*="color-theme-"]) + @property + a native color input. Own session.
color_system.migration_reality: APPLIED 2026-06-21 (claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2): consumers (layout/a11y/typography/scrolling/forms.css) migrated to the Phase-1 contract names; themes.css -> color-theme-66ccff.css (variable-overrides-only, @layer color-theme) created + linked AFTER color-scheme.css (themes.css KEPT per user, unlinked). Decisions: --txt-active/--txt-strong->--fg-emphasis (accent-hued, intentional look change); --button-bg/-hover->--bg-hover/--bg-selected; status (--error-*/--success) + form surfaces (--input-bg/--input-border) defined THEME-SIDE ONLY (no system color exists; color-scheme.css no-literals rule preserved); forms.css fully migrated + logged bugs fixed; decoration (body/html ::before) relocated to layout.css; form-label focus to forms.css. PAINTER REVERTED 2026-06-21 (same session, immediately after): removed the round-6 generic painter (label:has(>input:checked/:hover)) from color-scheme.css, keeping the --bg-selected/--bg-hover tokens; moved its ONLY effective paint (the header Light/Dark/System control pill -- every other state-machine control already had an unlayered consumer rule that won over it) into a layout.css header-label rule. REFINED 2026-06-21 (per user): the color-scheme CONTROL paint belongs in color-scheme.css (a color concern, not layout) -> moved it back, SCOPED to label:has(>input[name=color-scheme]) (governor owns its OWN control; still NOT nav/rows/buttons). ALSO fixed dead region panels: the migrated `app-container > *` bg rule had been NESTED inside `app-container{}` (resolved to `app-container app-container > *` -> never matched) -> removed it; header/main/aside/footer backgrounds now declared in color-scheme.css via a DEDICATED --bg-panel token (NOT --bg-muted -- user: no 'muted' behavior on layout elements; --bg-muted restored to its untouched contract default ButtonFace, applied to nothing): --bg-panel defaults to rgb(from currentColor r g b /5%), color-theme overrides to 10% accent, reduced-transparency collapses to ButtonFace. PENDING: human pixel-check.

demo_data_sources.what: Verified-free, browser-callable data APIs per demo (researched + CORS/auth verified live 2026-06-25; user-confirmed picks 2026-06-25). READ PATH = DIRECT from the static GitHub Pages front-end (no proxy) unless noted; favorites/notes are NOT an API concern (local app state or the shared account backend).
demo_data_sources.pokemon: PokeAPI REST v2 https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/ — no key, CORS '*' verified, BSD-3, no hard rate limit (cache + fair use); repo github.com/PokeAPI/pokeapi. PICTURES (user-required, CONFIRMED AVAILABLE): sprite PNGs at raw.githubusercontent.com/PokeAPI/sprites/master/sprites/pokemon/{id}.png (CORS '*', image/png verified) AND sprite URLs embedded in REST responses incl. official artwork (sprites.other.'official-artwork'.front_default); repo github.com/PokeAPI/sprites. VERDICT: direct client-side, no backend for read path.
demo_data_sources.bible: PRIMARY = AO Lab 'Free Use Bible API' https://bible.helloao.org/api/ — no key, CORS '*' verified, MIT, 1000+ translations (public-domain + permissive; NOT NIV/ESV); repo github.com/HelloAOLab/bible-api. FALLBACK = bible-api.com (no key, CORS '*', PD translations only, 15 req/30s). COPYRIGHTED-ONLY = API.Bible (scripture.api.bible) + ESV API (api.esv.org) require a SECRET key -> THIN PROXY required; use ONLY if NIV/ESV needed. VERDICT: direct for PD/permissive; proxy only for copyrighted.
demo_data_sources.crime_stats: PRIMARY/MAP = Fairfax County FCPD ArcGIS FeatureServers https://services9.arcgis.com/kYvfX7YK8OobHItA/arcgis/rest/services/ (e.g. CrimesAgainstProperty_FCPOD_FC/FeatureServer/0/query) — no key, CORS '*' verified, NATIVE lat/long/bbox querying verified (bbox -> 45,433 records); the location-map + alerting backbone; hub policedata-fcpdgis.hub.arcgis.com. STATEWIDE/AGENCY = FBI Crime Data Explorer https://api.usa.gov/crime/fbi/cde/ — FREE api.data.gov key (throttle token, NOT a true secret; ships client-side OK), CORS '*' verified; VSP + Fairfax PD by ORI; repo github.com/fbi-cde/crime-data-api; optional proxy only to hide the throttle key. VSP TABLES = data.virginia.gov CKAN https://data.virginia.gov/api/3/action/ — no key, CORS '*' verified; community-policing/UoF/UCR tables; weaker geo than ArcGIS. AVOID = VSP va.beyond2020.com (NO public REST API; scrape-only) -> use FBI CDE / data.virginia.gov for VSP instead. VERDICT: all three usable directly from the browser.

build_order: 1. Repo skeleton + PROGRESS.json + SessionStart hook + CLAUDE.md.
2. CSS base (starter set; reconcile dhcp concerns; inspect candidate-drops diag/stars/hero/_* and confirm with user before dropping).
3. index.html single universal scaffold (incl. dropdown-capable nav + its CSS).
5. Data shaping: schema(dhcp domain for now)/rules/ + concern-named modules (NEVER utils/helpers) - node tests.
7. CRUD UI forms + forms.css(de-data-*) + fallbacks.css + loading.css.
8. Wire dhcp datasets; full behavioral + regression pass in the enabled env.

_self.on_rotate: start progress/log-NNN.ndjson (next number), register it in shards[], update cursor.
_self.never: never edit past records; only append corrections (record type=lesson or note).
_self.resume: read PROGRESS.json first, then the newest shard, to restore context + why.

Then EXTENDED color-scheme.css per user: added --bg-selected + --bg-hover (both rgb(from AccentColor r g b / 5%)); --fg-link now = var(--fg-accent) (links=accent); generalized the active indicator into DOM-agnostic selected+hover for state-machine labels (label:has(>input:is([checkbox],[radio]):checked|:hover)); reduced-transparency collapses the tints to Highlight/ButtonFace. Deleted hydrateShell() + the isShellHydrated flag + 'Shell hydrated' log; inlined banner/nav-label/version injection as plain sequential calls in the SINGLE on-load function initializeOnInputLifecycle() (runs once from app.js; per-page data still loads via the nav radio's own oninput). Kept nav-label/banner/version text injection exactly as before. Renamed scheme->color-scheme everywhere (index.html name=color-scheme x3, color-scheme.css selectors+comments, oninput.js bindColorSchemeOnInput + selectors); storage key stays camelCase colorScheme. BROWSER-VERIFIED: API-down -> color-scheme persists/restores/round-trips; API-up -> nav labels inject + page data loads (10 rows, H1 Manage) + color-scheme 5/5. FUTURE NOTE: nav items will later be BUILT from JSON (like table cells) instead of a static skeleton. Round 4 (prior): BUGFIX (user-reported: choice not saving cross-session). ROOT CAUSE (reproduced + fixed in a REAL browser via Playwright/chromium): bindSchemeOnInput()+restoreColorScheme() ran AFTER `await hydrateShell()`, so a failed/slow data hydrate skipped them -> radios never bound to persistColorScheme, restore never ran; CSS color flip still worked (pure :has(:checked)) so it LOOKED fine but never persisted. FIX: moved both to run FIRST, BEFORE the await (color is pure UI, must not depend on the data API; scheme radios are static markup at parse time). BROWSER-VERIFIED both API-down (Light now persists+restores despite hydrate throwing) and API-up (5/5: Light saves+restores, System round-trips to OS-follow, no permanent override). Nav binding still gated behind hydrateShell (unchanged; nav needs data). Round 3 (prior): wired JS selection persistence + on-load restore into the EXISTING oninput.js init/storage (no new system). Added (mirroring the nav pair): bindSchemeOnInput() [persists each input[name=scheme].oninput value; NO lifecycle call, NO dispatchEvent], restoreColorScheme() [light/dark -> radio.checked=true, NO dispatch; absent/system -> nothing], getInitialColorScheme()/persistColorScheme() [read/write a colorScheme key BESIDE navigation in the autocss.app.v1 state via read/writePersistent]. Wired into initializeOnInputLifecycle() after the nav bind/trigger. JS sets only .checked, never styles; CSS reacts to :checked. node --check (ESM) passes. DEFERRED still: --txt/--bg/--accent->--fg/--bg consumer bridge; focus-indicator paint; BROWSER VERIFICATION. NOT yet browser-verified.

SAVE/DELETE DATA-MODEL DEFECT (HIGH-VALUE; own future session; recommended FIRST): Save succeeds (200) but writes to the WRONG LEVEL - POSTs a new top-level mockapi resource instead of editing record[0].items; Delete never hits the server. 3 causes: (1) read is nested (record[0].items) but write is flat (POST to collection root) - no path writes into record[0].items; (2) id-lookup bug querySelector('label > id') never matches the real <id-> cell (FACT #2) so Save always POSTs + Delete is DOM-only; (3) denormalize puts item field names at resource level. Affects ALL 12 endpoints. FIX = OPTION A (fix id selectors to 'label > id-', carry record[0]'s top-level id via a read accessor in oninput.js, splice item into record[0].items then PUT the whole resource) OR OPTION B (per-item backend routes). Full analysis: NDJSON 2026-06-10 lesson + SESSION-HANDOFF LEDGER. Old Q2 RESOLVED -> FIX (not keep-faithful).
